meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn to implement a lean analytics stack using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate features and crush the 'no project ideas' trap for good.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing ideas in a vacuum. Implement a lean analytics stack to track user behavior on your MVP. By measuring friction points, you transform 'no ideas' into data-driven feature requests. Use Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code to automate the instrumentation, turning user engagement into your product roadmap.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with mocking rhythmic precision. You have the technical skill to build anything, but you’re paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' syndrome—a silent killer that drains founder momentum faster than technical debt. You aren't lacking creativity; you're lacking a feedback loop.

Real products aren't born from sudden sparks of genius; they are chiseled from the raw data of user behavior. By installing a robust analytics layer today, you stop guessing and start observing. This tutorial shows you how to stop building for ghosts and start building for metrics that actually scale.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that signals value. This prevents feature bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Product Architect. Help me define a North Star metric for a B2B SaaS MVP that solves user churn. Provide a schema for tracking this metric via PostHog.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking plan markdown file based on the North Star metric defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate your provider early. We use PostHog for its balance of power and ease.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript wrapper for PostHog initialization in a Next.js environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'posthog-js' and 'posthog-node' and implement the provider wrapper.

steps.title: Instrument High-Intent Events
steps.description: Don't track clicks; track intent. Instrument the sign-up flow and your core value-add feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom hook useAnalytics that wraps PostHog.capture() with standardized event properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Instrument the user sign-up and dashboard entry points using the useAnalytics hook.'

steps.title: Automate Data Feedback Loops
steps.description: Set up alerts so you know when users drop off, identifying your next feature opportunity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a SQL query for PostHog to identify where users drop off in a multi-step onboarding funnel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure a cron job that pings a Slack webhook if onboarding conversion drops below 20%.'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Usage Data
steps.description: Use the data to kill unused features and double down on what works.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this CSV export of user feature usage and suggest three high-impact features to build next.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a roadmap document based on the current analytics dashboard trends.'

pro_tips: Never track everything. If you track every click, you'll drown in noise. Track only 3-5 'conversion' events.
Use 'Feature Flags' via your analytics tool to toggle features on/off without redeploying code.
Anonymous tracking is sufficient for MVPs. Don't waste time on complex identity resolution until you have 100+ daily active users.
Set up a 'Waitlist' event immediately. Even if your feature isn't built, tracking clicks on a 'Coming Soon' button validates demand.
Automate your reporting. If you have to manually check your dashboard, you will eventually stop checking it.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment, invite 10 users, and monitor the funnel for 48 hours. Use the data to validate your first pivot.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping features that users actually crave. Our community of founders is waiting to help you scale your next big win. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
